mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, indicating severe labor market slack, consumer spending and corporate earnings weaken, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 8%, the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in industrial production and retail sales signals synchronized expansion in both supply and demand, reinforcing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1%
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 7%), declines in the federal funds rate are highly stimulative for equities due to large output gaps and accommodative monetary policy. In a low unemployment regime, rate cuts are less effective or may signal economic weakness, leading to negative equity responses.
failure_condition: In high unemployment periods, the 60-day forward return following a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) or is negative, rejecting the hypothesized positive effect.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth vs 60-day forward return is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average return in the top quartile of RSAFS growth is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening economic activity and supply constraints, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime, but more pronounced during recessions
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase > 0.2%) implies falling real interest rates, which reduces discount rates on equities and boosts short-term (5-day) returns.
regime_hint: low interest rate environment with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recovery phase (HOUST between 700 and 1000 and rising month-over-month), increasing housing starts signal improving construction activity and consumer wealth, leading to positive equity returns over 20 trading days. Outside this phase, the effect is weak or negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST between 700 and 1000 and HOUST month-over-month positive
regime_hint: housing recovery phase (HOUST 700-1000 with positive momentum)
failure_condition: In the defined regime, the 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or is negative in more than 50% of instances.
